mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary conditions, which reduces the cost of capital for businesses and encourages investment, thereby supporting equity valuations and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 1.5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained low unemployment rate indicates a tight labor market, which typically translates into strong consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as economic growth continues.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.5% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If, when UNRATE is below 4.5% for 3 months, the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.10) over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and investment, signaling broad economic strength that boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.3% and HOUST MoM growth > 2% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The correlation between the combined signal (RSAFS and HOUST growth) and 20-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates increasing manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, suggesting strong underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 0.2% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If, when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.2% for two months, the S&P 500's 5-day forward returns are negative on average over a 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Robust quarterly GDP growth signifies a healthy and expanding economy, which typically leads to improved corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2.5% for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth meets the trigger condition over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a growing economy and strong labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return is negative when PAYEMS shows positive MoM growth over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation coupled with increasing federal funds rates signals a tightening monetary policy to combat overheating, which typically increases discount rates and reduces future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS has increased by at least 25bps over the last 3 months
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances despite the negative signal over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer confidence and spending, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues for consumer-facing sectors and supports overall market performance, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If, when RSAFS growth meets the trigger, the S&P 500's 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-statistic < 1.5) over a 3-year backtest.
